content.cn_text: 你将听到一段为初学者准备的航海课。
content.en_text: You will hear part of a lesson about sailing for beginners.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间阅读问题31-40[暂停1分钟]
content.en_text: First you have some time to look at questions 31-40 [Pause 1 minute]

content.cn_text: 现在仔细听录音，回答问题31-40。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31-40.

content.cn_text: 如果你知道自己在做什么，即使是一个基本的漂浮结构，比如用一块亚麻布绑在临时桅杆上的木筏，也能在风中航行。
content.en_text: Even a basic floating structure like a log raft with a piece of linen attached to a makeshift mast will sail before the wind if you know what you are doing.

content.cn_text: 而在其他方向航行则完全是另一回事了。
content.en_text: Now sailing in other directions is a different story altogether though.

content.cn_text: 为了向多个方向航行，船的设计和操纵必须使风的力量使它穿过风或进入风，并随着风移动。
content.en_text: To sail in multiple directions, a boat must be designed and rigged so that the force of the wind moves it across the wind or into the wind, as well as moving it with the wind.

content.cn_text: 这个设计和索具过程可以是相当复杂的，但幸运的是，没有人会担心，因为我们所有的船都准备好了。
content.en_text: This design and rigging process can be quite complicated but luckily, none of you will have to worry about that as all our boats are primed for the off.

content.cn_text: 你们需要关注的是控制方向，学习一些基本的动作;这两个方面的帆船将形成你的核心训练，在未来几周的课程。
content.en_text: What you guys need to focus on is controlling your direction and learning some of the basic manoeuvres; these two aspects of sailing will form the core of your training over the coming weeks on this course.

content.cn_text: 让我们从方向控制开始。
content.en_text: Let's start with direction control.

content.cn_text: 一艘没有控制手段的船，无论指向哪个方向，都会直接顺风行驶。
content.en_text: A boat with no means of control will travel straight downwind no matter what direction it's pointing in.

content.cn_text: 因此，学习控制航行方向的第一步是使用方向舵来抵消风对帆的自然影响。
content.en_text: Using a rudder to counteract the wind's natural effect on the sail is the first step, therefore, in learning to control the direction you sail in.

content.cn_text: 有了舵，船首就可以指向你想要的任何方向。
content.en_text: With a rudder, the bow of the boat can be pointed in any direction you desire.

content.cn_text: 但是我们需要的不仅仅是一个舵，否则当船在风中移动时，没有什么可以阻止它向侧面滑动。
content.en_text: But we need more than just a rudder, otherwise there is nothing to stop the boat from sliding sideways when it is moving across the wind.

content.cn_text: 这就是龙骨或者
content.en_text: That's where the keel or

content.cn_text: 中心板进来，这将防止任何不必要的侧向运动。
content.en_text: centreboard comes in This will prevent any unwanted sideward movement.

content.cn_text: 在我们的船上，我们使用一个可拆卸的中心板。
content.en_text: In our boats we use a removable centreboard.

content.cn_text: 为什么?
content.en_text: Why?

content.cn_text: 因为这能让他们在浅水区航行。
content.en_text: Because this enables them to sail in shallow waters.

content.cn_text: 如果你的活动板是固定的，那么当你遇到不是很深的水域时，你的船就会搁浅。
content.en_text: If your centreboard is fixed in place permanently then your boat will ground when you encounter areas of water which aren't very deep.

content.cn_text: 有了舵和中板来控制方向，你的船现在可以在许多方向上行驶了!
content.en_text: With the rudder for steering and the centreboard to prevent sideward movement, your boat is now capable of travelling in numerous directions!

content.cn_text: 在三种基本的航海动作中，(1)顺风航行，(2)迎风航行，(3)迎风航行，后者是迄今为止最难的。
content.en_text: Of the three basic sailing manoeuvres, which are: (1) sailing with the wind, (2)sailing across the wind and (3) sailing into the wind the latter of the three is by far the hardest.

content.cn_text: 迎风航行叫做迎风航向。
content.en_text: Sailing into the wind is called tacking to windward.

content.cn_text: 实际上，没有船能直接迎风航行。
content.en_text: In reality, no boat can actually sail directly into the wind.

content.cn_text: 如果它这样做，它的帆将开始拍打并变得无用。
content.en_text: If it does so, its sail will start to flap and become useless.

content.cn_text: 问题36:这就是钉球的由来;船沿之字形航线逆风航行。
content.en_text: That's where tacking comes in; boats sail upwind by following a zig-zag course.

content.cn_text: 在你的帆开始拍打之前，你通常可以在风向的45度范围内航行，这就是我们在航向时的目标，因为风很少以相同的力量和方向吹，持续地迎风航行需要很高的技巧。
content.en_text: You can generally head to within 45 degrees of the wind direction before your sail starts to flap and this is what we aim to do when we tack Because the wind seldom blows with the same force and in the same direction consistently sailing into the wind requires great skill.

content.cn_text: 熟练的水手在航行过程中学会感知风向的细微变化，并据此做出微小的调整。
content.en_text: Skilled sailors learn to sense the subtle changes in wind direction as they sail along and make minor adjustments for these.

content.cn_text: 在你能有效地做到这一点之前，你需要大量的练习。
content.en_text: It will take you a lot of practice before you can do this effectively.

content.cn_text: 逆风航行叫做到达。
content.en_text: Sailing across the wind is called reaching.

content.cn_text: 船只往往在到达目的地时以最快速度航行。
content.en_text: Boats tend to clock their fastest speeds when reaching.

content.cn_text: 迎风航行被称为奔跑，但是，与人们所期望的相反，奔跑并不像到达一样快。
content.en_text: Sailing with the wind is called running, but, contrary to what people might expect, running is not as fast as reaching.

content.cn_text: 这是因为水手不能自己制造风阻力;产生多大的阻力完全取决于推动风帆的风的强度。
content.en_text: This is because the sailor is not able to create his own wind resistance; how much resistance is created is entirely down to the strength of the wind which is pushing against the sail.

content.cn_text: 今天下午，我们将上第一节实践课。
content.en_text: This afternoon, we'll have our first practical lesson.

content.cn_text: 不过，你们要先在陆地上熟悉你们的船，直到明天才会把它们带到水面上，而且只有在天气允许的情况下。
content.en_text: You will get to know your boats on dry land first though, and will not be taking them out onto the water until tomorrow,and only then if weather permitting.

content.cn_text: 我们休息半小时左右吃午饭，好吗?
content.en_text: Let's break for lunch now for half an hour or SO, okay?

content.cn_text: 旁白:这是第四部分的结尾。
content.en_text: Narrator: That is the end of Section 4.

content.cn_text: 你现在有半分钟的时间检查答案。
content.en_text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: [暂停30秒]
content.en_text: [Pause 30 seconds]

content.cn_text: 听力测试到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of the listening test.

content.cn_text: 你现在有十分钟的时间把答案誊写在听力答题卡上。
content.en_text: You now have ten minutes to transfer your answers to the Listening Answer Sheet.

groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: What aspects of sailing will the course concentrate on?

options.title: B
options.content: direction control and performing basic boat movements

options.title: C
options.content: complex manoeuvres and control of direction

items.analysis: 文中明确指出"controlling your direction"和"learning some of the basic manoeuvres"是学生需要专注的两个方面，并强调这两个方面将构成课程训练的核心。这直接对应了选项B中的"direction control and performing basic boat movements"，因此B是正确答案。

items.title: What is the purpose of a rudder?

options.title: C
options.content: to act like a natural wind effect

items.analysis: 文中明确指出使用rudder（船舵）是为了控制航行方向。原文说"Using a rudder to counteract the wind's natural effect on the sail is the first step, therefore, in learning to control the direction you sail in"，这直接表明了rudder的作用是用来控制航行方向的。选项B中的"improve direction control"与原文的"control the direction"意思一致，因此B是正确答案。

items.title: What does a centreboard prevent?

options.title: C
options.content: boats from sinking in shallow water

items.analysis: 文中明确指出中央板（centreboard）的功能是防止任何不想要的侧向移动（prevent any unwanted sideward movement）。这直接对应了选项B中的"undesirable sideways movement"。因此，B选项是正确答案。

items.title: Why is it better to have a removable centreboard?

options.title: A
options.content: in order to travel in deep water

options.title: B
options.content: in order to be able to ground the boat in shallow water

options.title: C
options.content: in order to prevent the boat from getting stuck

items.analysis: 文中解释了使用可拆卸中央板的原因是为了能在浅水中航行。如果中央板是固定的，船只在浅水区域会搁浅。因此，可拆卸的中央板实际上是为了防止船只在浅水中搁浅或被卡住。这与C选项"为了防止船只被卡住"最为符合，因为它概括了可拆卸中央板的主要功能。

items.title: Which of the three basic manoeuvres are the most difficult?
items.analysis: 文中明确指出三种基本帆船操作中，最后提到的"sailing into the wind"（逆风航行）是"by far the hardest"（迄今为止最难的）。这直接回答了题目所问的"哪种基本操作最困难"。此外，文中还进一步解释了逆风航行的难度，包括需要采用"tacking"（抢风航行）技巧，以及需要高超的技能来应对风向和风力的细微变化。这些解释进一步证实了逆风航行的难度最高。

items.title: What is tacking?

options.title: C
options.content: sailing upwind in a zig-zag direction

items.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为原文明确指出tacking是一种逆风航行的技术，而且船只是通过沿之字形路线航行来实现的。文中说"boats sail upwind by following a zig-zag course"，这直接对应了选项C中的"sailing upwind in a zig-zag direction"。此外，原文还解释了为什么需要采用这种方法：直接逆风航行是不可能的，因为帆会失去作用。

items.title: What happens if you sail your boat too directly into the wind?

options.title: B
options.content: it is hard to keep your boat travelling in a consistent direction

options.title: C
options.content: your boat will start to tack

items.analysis: 文中明确指出，当船直接迎风航行时，船帆会开始拍打并变得无用。这直接对应了选项A"the sail stops working properly"（船帆停止正常工作）。船帆拍打和变得无用意味着它不能正常发挥作用，因此A选项最准确地描述了直接迎风航行的后果。

items.title: When do boats sail at their fastest speeds?
items.analysis: 文中明确指出船只在横风航行（reaching）时达到最快速度。这直接回答了题目所问的问题。材料还进一步解释了为什么顺风航行（running）不如横风航行快，因为顺风航行时船只无法创造自己的风阻，速度完全取决于风力大小。这些信息共同支持了B选项"while reaching"是正确答案。

items.title: It is implied that sailors have more control over the amount of wind resistance that they get from their sails when they are
items.analysis: 文中明确指出横风航行（reaching）时船只能达到最快速度，这表明在这种航行方式下，水手能够最有效地利用和控制风阻。相比之下，顺风航行时水手对风阻的控制较少，而逆风航行虽然需要高超技巧，但并未提及更好地控制风阻。因此，横风航行（reaching）是水手能够最好地控制风阻的航行方式。

items.title: When will the people on the course get to go sailing for the first time?

options.title: B
options.content: at the end of their first practical lesson

options.title: C
options.content: the next day if the weather is suitable

items.analysis: 文中明确指出学员将在陆地上熟悉船只，直到第二天才会真正出海，而且还要视天气情况而定。这与选项C完全吻合，表明学员第一次真正出海是在第二天，并且取决于天气情况。
